is an Indian violinist who performs [Hindustani classical](/wiki/Hindustani_classical "Hindustani classical") music and fusion. She is the grand-daughter of the renowned Padmabhushan Dr. [N. Rajam](/wiki/N._Rajam "N. Rajam").[[2]](#cite_note-2) and the daughter of Dr. [Sangeeta Shankar](/wiki/Sangeeta_Shankar "Sangeeta Shankar")[[3]](#cite_note-3)
[*Theatre de la Ville, Paris*](https://www.theatredelaville-paris.com/en/spectacles/saison-2021-2022/musiques/sangeeta-shankar-and-daughters)

Musical artist

**Ragini Shankar** is an Indian violinist who performs [Hindustani classical](/wiki/Hindustani_classical "Hindustani classical") music and fusion. She is the grand-daughter of the renowned Padmabhushan Dr. [N. Rajam](/wiki/N._Rajam "N. Rajam").[[2]](#cite_note-2) and the daughter of Dr. [Sangeeta Shankar](/wiki/Sangeeta_Shankar "Sangeeta Shankar")[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ragini_Shankar&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

She began her training at the age of 4 and gave her first public performance as an 11-year-old.[[4]](#cite_note-Official_Website-4) She plays violin in the [*Gayaki Ang*](/wiki/Gharana "Gharana"), a revolutionary technique to reproduce vocal music on the violin.

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ragini_Shankar&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Education")]

Shankar excelled in her education in mechanical engineering and also holds a master's degree in music.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Performing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ragini_Shankar&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Performing career")]

She has performed in various prestigious festivals like the [Europalia](/wiki/Europalia "Europalia"),[[6]](#cite_note-6) [MERU, Netherlands](/w/index.php?title=MERU,_Netherlands&action=edit&redlink=1 "MERU, Netherlands (page does not exist)"),[[7]](#cite_note-MERU-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) [Sawai Gandharva Bhimsen Festival](/wiki/Sawai_Gandharva_Bhimsen_Festival "Sawai Gandharva Bhimsen Festival"),[[9]](#cite_note-9) [Dover Lane Music Conference](/wiki/Dover_Lane_Music_Conference "Dover Lane Music Conference"),[[10]](#cite_note-10) [Saptak Festival of Music](/wiki/Saptak_Festival_of_Music "Saptak Festival of Music"),[[11]](#cite_note-11) [Theatre de la Ville, Paris](/w/index.php?title=Theatre_de_la_Ville,_Paris&action=edit&redlink=1 "Theatre de la Ville, Paris (page does not exist)"),[[12]](#cite_note-12) [Theatre de l'Alhambra, Geneva, Switzerland](/w/index.php?title=Theatre_de_l%27Alhambra,_Geneva,_Switzerland&action=edit&redlink=1 "Theatre de l'Alhambra, Geneva, Switzerland (page does not exist)"),[[13]](#cite_note-auto3-13) [MilapFest](/w/index.php?title=MilapFest&action=edit&redlink=1 "MilapFest (page does not exist)"),[[14]](#cite_note-14) Yaksha (festival),[[15]](#cite_note-15) Aarohi for Pancham Nishad,[[16]](#cite_note-16) Jaya Smriti organised by [Hema Malini](/wiki/Hema_Malini "Hema Malini"),[[17]](#cite_note-17) Temple of Fine Arts,[[18]](#cite_note-18) Bengal Music Foundation.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

She has performed in several countries abroad; the USA,[[20]](#cite_note-20) Canada,[[21]](#cite_note-21) UK,[[22]](#cite_note-22) Netherlands,[[7]](#cite_note-MERU-7) Switzerland,[[13]](#cite_note-auto3-13) Germany, France,[[23]](#cite_note-23) Belgium,[[24]](#cite_note-24) Hungary, Singapore,[[25]](#cite_note-25) Malaysia and Dubai.[[26]](#cite_note-26) Ragini gives lecture demonstrations and conducts workshops on a regular basis during her travels.

She is known for her collaborations with various artists in diverse genres. Ragini and her
sister [Nandini Shankar](/wiki/Nandini_Shankar "Nandini Shankar") have been signed by [Decca Records](/wiki/Decca_Records "Decca Records") US, a part of [Universal Music](/wiki/Universal_Music "Universal Music")
Group for a full-length music album for their musical ensemble ‘Taraana’.[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28)
She is a part of the renowned Bollywood lyricist [Irshad Kamil](/wiki/Irshad_Kamil "Irshad Kamil")’s The Ink Band, a series of poetry interwoven with music.[[29]](#cite_note-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30) She is also a part of an Indo-French musical project called Sangata, formed by the noted French composer [Thierry Pecou](/wiki/Thierry_Pecou "Thierry Pecou")[[31]](#cite_note-31) and was covered in an article in *[Le Monde](/wiki/Le_Monde "Le Monde")*.[[32]](#cite_note-32) Her recent collaborations include inStrings, an innovative fusion band, giving a new sound to popular Indian tunes.[[33]](#cite_note-33)

Ragini is a
recipient of the distinguished Aditya Birla Kala Kiran Award[[34]](#cite_note-auto2-34) and the Jashn-e-Youngistan [[35]](#cite_note-auto1-35)
[[36]](#cite_note-auto-36) Award presented by the Vice President of India.
She was a faculty at [Whistling Woods International](/wiki/Whistling_Woods_International "Whistling Woods International") School of Music in Mumbai for 6 years and currently teaches at the Rajam School of Violin.[[37]](#cite_note-37) She has appeared on the platform of [Talks at Google](/wiki/Talks_at_Google "Talks at Google")[[38]](#cite_note-38) and [Tedx](/wiki/Tedx "Tedx")[[39]](#cite_note-39) for her talks on Indian music.

She resides in Mumbai.

## Awards and honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ragini_Shankar&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Awards and honours")]

Aditya Birla Kala Kiran Award,presented by [Rajashree Birla](/wiki/Rajashree_Birla "Rajashree Birla"), 2019[[34]](#cite_note-auto2-34)

Jashn-e-Youngistan presented by the Vice-President of India, [Venkaiah Naidu](/wiki/Venkaiah_Naidu "Venkaiah Naidu"), 2018[[35]](#cite_note-auto1-35)
[[36]](#cite_note-auto-36)

Jaya Smriti presented by film actress and dancer, [Hema Malini](/wiki/Hema_Malini "Hema Malini"), 2012
